name: Cross-site Scripting (XSS)
description: The application does not properly neutralize user-controllable input before placing it in output that is used as a web page. This allows attackers to inject malicious scripts.
impact: Session hijacking, credential theft, website defacement, malware distribution.
mitigation: Input validation, output encoding, Content Security Policy (CSP), HTTPOnly cookies.

name: SQL Injection
description: The application constructs SQL queries using unsanitized user input. Attackers can inject SQL commands to access, modify, or delete data.
impact: Data breach, data manipulation, privilege escalation, full database compromise.
mitigation: Parameterized queries, prepared statements, ORM usage, input validation, least-privilege database accounts.

name: OS Command Injection
description: The application passes unsanitized user input to a system shell. Attackers can execute arbitrary operating system commands.
impact: Full server compromise, data exfiltration, lateral movement.
mitigation: Avoid shell commands, use library functions, input validation, sandboxing.

name: Path Traversal
description: The application uses user input to construct file paths without proper sanitization. Attackers can access files outside the intended directory.
impact: Sensitive file disclosure, configuration leak, source code theft.
mitigation: Canonicalize paths, use chroot, whitelist allowed files, validate input.

name: Improper Authentication
description: The application does not properly verify the identity of users. Authentication mechanisms can be bypassed.
impact: Unauthorized access, account takeover, privilege escalation.
mitigation: Multi-factor authentication, strong password policies, secure session management.

name: Missing Authorization
description: The application does not check whether a user is authorized to perform an action. Users can access functionality they should not have.
impact: Data exposure, unauthorized operations, privilege escalation.
mitigation: Implement access controls, use RBAC, verify permissions on every request.

name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
description: The application makes HTTP requests based on user input without validation. Attackers can make the server request internal resources.
impact: Internal network scanning, cloud metadata theft, internal service exploitation.
mitigation: URL validation, allowlist domains, disable redirects, network segmentation.

name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
description: The application deserializes data from untrusted sources. Malicious serialized objects can execute arbitrary code.
impact: Remote code execution, denial of service.
mitigation: Avoid native deserialization, use JSON, implement integrity checks.

name: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
description: The application does not verify that a request was intentionally sent by the user. Attackers can trick users into performing unintended actions.
impact: Unauthorized actions, state changes, fund transfers.
mitigation: Anti-CSRF tokens, SameSite cookies, Referer header validation.

name: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
description: A critical function in the application can be accessed without authentication. Administrative functions are exposed.
mitigation: Require authentication for all sensitive endpoints, API key verification.

name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
description: The application contains hard-coded passwords, API keys, or cryptographic keys in source code.
impact: Unauthorized access, cannot change credentials without code update.
mitigation: Use environment variables, secrets management, key rotation.

name: XML External Entity (XXE)
description: The XML parser processes external entity references in XML input. Attackers can read files, perform SSRF, or cause DoS.
impact: File disclosure, SSRF, denial of service.
mitigation: Disable external entities, use JSON, update XML parsers.

name: Unrestricted File Upload
description: The application allows users to upload files without proper validation. Attackers can upload malicious files (webshells).
mitigation: Validate file type, use allowlists, store outside webroot, rename files.

name: Information Exposure
description: The application exposes sensitive information through error messages, headers, or responses that should be restricted.
impact: Information gathering for attackers, credential exposure.
mitigation: Custom error pages, remove sensitive headers, log errors server-side only.

name: Improper Privilege Management
description: The application does not properly manage user privileges. Users can escalate their access level.
mitigation: Principle of least privilege, regular privilege audits, RBAC.
